metadata.title: Munin — agent-native business apps
metadata.description: Open-source headless business app suite (Knowledge Base, Conversations, CRM, CMS) where the AI agent is the UI.

common.networkError: Network error — is the API reachable?

home.title: Munin
home.tagline: Agent-native business apps. The AI agent is the UI.
home.subtitle: Knowledge Base · Conversations · CRM · CMS — open source, MCP-first.

signUp.title: Create your account
signUp.subtitle: Munin — agent-native business apps.
signUp.invitationInvalid: This invitation is no longer valid (revoked or expired).
signUp.invitationLookupFailed: Could not look up the invitation.

overview.title: Welcome to Munin
overview.subtitle: Connect your AI agent or build a server-side integration.

connectCard.title: Connect your AI agent
connectCard.description: Add this URL to Claude Desktop, Cursor, or any MCP client.
connectCard.consentNote: You'll see a consent screen here when an agent connects.

buildCard.title: Build a server integration
buildCard.description: Voice AI, web chatbot, or mobile app? Mint short-lived end-user tokens server-side.

agentStatus.title: Self-service agent
agentStatus.connected: {count, plural, one {1 agent connected} other {# agents connected}} — replies to end-user messages will be handled automatically.
agentStatus.notConnected: No self-service agent connected. End-user messages will sit until a human replies from the dashboard or an agent runner is connected.
agentStatus.staleNotConnected: No self-service agent connected, and there are unanswered end-user messages. Reply from the dashboard or connect an agent runner.
agentStatus.hint: Connect an agent: run @getmunin/agent-runtime against this Munin's MCP, or use the cloud product's auto-replier.

review.title: Review
review.subtitle: KB suggestions, CRM merges, and outreach drafts proposed by curators. Approve to publish/send, dismiss to discard.
review.automateHint: Fully automate this by connecting an <adminAgent>Admin Agent</adminAgent>.

outreach.title: Outreach campaigns
outreach.subtitle: Operator-defined outbound email campaigns. Each enabled campaign produces curator-drafted initials weekly; approve each draft from the Review surface before it sends. Suppression and consent are re-checked at approve-time.
outreach.needsEmailChannel: Create an email channel under Channels first — outreach campaigns send via your configured SMTP.
outreach.needsSegment: Create a CRM segment first — campaigns target a saved segment of consenting contacts.
outreach.emptyBody: A campaign needs a name, a one-paragraph brief, a CRM segment as audience, and an email channel. New campaigns are off by default; flip Enable when you're ready for the curator to start drafting.
outreach.createDescription: Drafts won't send automatically — every initial lands in the Review queue for human approval first.
outreach.briefHint: One paragraph in plain English describing what you want to say and why. The curator personalises per contact, grounding in your KB.
outreach.briefPlaceholder: We just shipped self-serve onboarding for B2B teams. We'd like to talk to ops leaders at companies that recently raised, to learn how they're approaching this …

outreachDrafts.emptyBody: When the draft-initial curator runs against an enabled campaign, drafts land here for approval. Approve to send via the campaign's email channel; dismiss to discard.
outreachDrafts.editComingSoon: Inline editing ships next — for now, dismiss and have the curator redraft, or rerun the campaign.

errors.approve: Could not approve draft (may be blocked by suppression / consent).

kbCandidates.emptyBody: Curators will draft KB candidates from resolved handovers and similar signals. Approved candidates publish to your chosen space.
kbCandidates.dismissConfirm: Dismiss this candidate? It will be deleted.

crmMergeProposals.title: CRM merge proposals
crmMergeProposals.subtitle: Pending duplicate-contact merges proposed by the CRM hygiene curator. Apply to atomically copy the recommended patch onto the keeper, reassign activities and deals, and archive the duplicate. Dismiss to record that the pair is not the same person.
crmMergeProposals.emptyBody: The CRM hygiene curator proposes merges when it finds duplicate contacts. Approve to atomically merge keeper + duplicate, or dismiss to keep them separate.
crmMergeProposals.hint: The CRM hygiene curator runs weekly and re-files proposals if it finds the same suspect pair again — dismissing one prevents that.

agents.title: Connected agents
agents.subtitle: Every OAuth-authorized agent and end-user token issued for this org. Revoke any active token to immediately invalidate further MCP calls.
agents.emptyBody: Add the MCP URL to Claude Desktop, Cursor, or your runtime, then complete consent — issued tokens will appear here.

channels.title: Channels
channels.subtitle: Inbound surfaces where end-users reach you. Today: chat widget and email. Voice via MCP setup tools.
channels.createWidgetDescription: Mints a `mn_widget_*` API key bound to this channel. The widget loads on the configured origins and posts via /api/conv/widget/messages.
channels.originsHint: Comma- or whitespace-separated list of origins (full URLs with scheme + host) that may load the widget. Leave empty to allow any origin.
channels.createdTitle: Channel created — copy the widget key now
channels.createdDescription: Widget key for {name}. We only store the hash; we can't show it again.
channels.rotateConfirm: Rotate the widget key for {name}? Existing keys will be revoked.
channels.rotatedDescription: New widget key for {name}. Update your widget config and dispatch.
channels.emptyBody: Add a chat widget or email channel to start collecting messages.

email.createDescription: Bring your own SMTP credentials (and optionally IMAP for inbound replies). Passwords are encrypted server-side; we never show them again.

apiKeys.title: API keys
apiKeys.subtitle: Long-lived admin keys for server-to-server integrations and programmatic admin agents. Self-hosters and CI use these too. Treat them like passwords.
apiKeys.createDescription: The plaintext value is shown ONCE, immediately after creation. Copy it now and store it in your secret manager.
apiKeys.emptyBody: Create your first key above to get started.
apiKeys.createdDescription: Munin only stores the hash. We can't show you this key again.

team.title: Team
team.subtitle: Members and pending invitations for this org. Owners can invite others, change roles, and remove members; members can use the apps but can't manage access.
team.inviteDescription: They'll get an email with a link to accept. The link expires in 7 days.
team.manualShareTitle: Email isn't configured — share this link manually
team.manualShareBody: No email provider is set up on this Munin instance, so we didn't send an email. Send the link below to {email} yourself, or set MUNIN_MAIL_PROVIDER (e.g. resend) and restart so future invites mail automatically.
team.removeConfirm: Remove this member? They lose access to the org immediately.
team.invitesEmptyBody: Use the form above to invite someone — they'll appear here until accepted.

endUsers.title: End-users
endUsers.subtitle: People your customer-facing agents act on behalf of. Created server-side via the SDK or /api/end-users/lookup; never trusted from agent input.
endUsers.emptyBody: Mint your first delegated end-user token via POST /api/delegated-token with an admin key from your backend, and the corresponding EndUser will appear here.
endUsers.noTokensRevoked: No active tokens to revoke for this end-user.

usage.title: Usage
usage.subtitle: MCP tool calls counted against your tier limits. Counters reset at the start of each window.

auditLog.title: Audit log
auditLog.subtitle: Every tool call and control-plane mutation, newest first. Filter by tool, actor type, or correlation id to isolate one request chain.
auditLog.filterDescription: Leave blank to match all values.
auditLog.emptyBody: Tool calls and control-plane writes appear here. Connect an agent and try a tool.

export.title: Data export
export.subtitle: Download a complete JSON dump of your org's domain data — knowledge base, end-users, and configuration. Use this to migrate to a self-hosted Munin or to keep an offline copy.
export.cardDescription: Includes: organization, end-users, agents, and KB spaces / documents / versions. Excludes: tokens, API keys, and the audit log (those are operational data you can't restore from an export).

profile.title: Edit your details
profile.subtitle: Update the name shown on your account.

acceptInvite.acceptedBody: You're now a member of the org.

errors.lookup: Could not look up the invitation.
errors.expired: This invitation is no longer valid (revoked or expired).

errors.SIGNUP_NOT_ALLOWED: Signup is restricted on this Munin instance. Ask an admin to invite you.
errors.SIGNUP_DOMAIN_NOT_ALLOWED: Your email domain isn't on the allowlist, and there's no pending invitation for this address. Ask an admin to invite you.
errors.SIGNUP_INVITE_ONLY: Signup is invite-only on this Munin instance. Ask an admin to send you an invitation.
errors.INVITATION_EMAIL_MISMATCH: This invitation is for a different email address.
errors.UNAUTHORIZED: You need to sign in to do that.
errors.FORBIDDEN: You don't have permission to do that.
errors.RATE_LIMITED: Too many requests. Please slow down and try again.
errors.QUOTA_EXCEEDED: You've hit a quota limit. Upgrade or wait for it to reset.
errors.VALIDATION_ERROR: The data you submitted didn't validate.
errors.FEEDBACK_RATE_LIMIT: Too many suggestions created in the last hour. Try again later.

terms.title: Terms of Use — Munin
terms.description: Terms governing use of getmunin.com and the Munin open-source project.
